analysis

Loading and Cleaning

load("severity.RData")

# Dropping rows with missing values and ID variable
dat <- na.omit(dat) %>%
  select(-id)

# Factoring & Labeling

dat$gender <- factor(dat$gender, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Female", "Male"))
dat$vaccine <- factor(dat$vaccine, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("Unvaccinated", "Vaccinated"))
dat$hypertension <- factor(dat$hypertension, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
dat$diabetes <- factor(dat$diabetes, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("No", "Yes"))
dat$severity <- factor(dat$severity, levels = c(0, 1), labels = c("not_severe", "severe"))
dat$race <- factor(dat$race, levels = c(1, 2, 3, 4), labels = c("White", "Asian", "Black", "Hispanic"))
dat$smoking <- factor(dat$smoking, levels = c(0, 1, 2), labels = c("Never", "Former", "Current"))

Data preperation and partitioning for modeling

# Random 1000 sample with Uni
set.seed(3015)
my_dat <- dat[sample(nrow(dat), 1000), ]

# 80/20 split
set.seed(3015)
data_split <- initial_split(my_dat, prop = 0.80)
training_data <- training(data_split)
testing_data <- testing(data_split)

Summary Statistics

summary(my_dat)
##       age          gender          race        smoking        height     
##  Min.   :48.0   Female:524   White   :633   Never  :636   Min.   :152.9  
##  1st Qu.:57.0   Male  :476   Asian   : 51   Former :275   1st Qu.:166.6  
##  Median :60.0                Black   :222   Current: 89   Median :170.3  
##  Mean   :60.2                Hispanic: 94                 Mean   :170.2  
##  3rd Qu.:63.0                                             3rd Qu.:173.9  
##  Max.   :75.0                                             Max.   :187.2  
##      weight            bmi        diabetes  hypertension      SBP       
##  Min.   : 56.10   Min.   :17.50   No :860   No :515      Min.   :103.0  
##  1st Qu.: 74.90   1st Qu.:25.70   Yes:140   Yes:485      1st Qu.:125.0  
##  Median : 79.60   Median :27.60                          Median :130.0  
##  Mean   : 79.69   Mean   :27.56                          Mean   :130.4  
##  3rd Qu.: 84.60   3rd Qu.:29.40                          3rd Qu.:136.0  
##  Max.   :102.10   Max.   :36.60                          Max.   :156.0  
##       LDL                vaccine      depression           severity  
##  Min.   : 51.0   Unvaccinated:395   Min.   : 0.000   not_severe:677  
##  1st Qu.: 98.0   Vaccinated  :605   1st Qu.: 6.000   severe    :323  
##  Median :112.0                      Median : 7.000                   
##  Mean   :111.8                      Mean   : 6.953                   
##  3rd Qu.:125.0                      3rd Qu.: 8.000                   
##  Max.   :185.0                      Max.   :12.000
my_dat %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = severity, 
    statistic = list(all_continuous() ~ "{mean} ({sd})", 
                     all_categorical() ~ "{n} ({p}%)"),
    digits = all_continuous() ~ 1,
    label = list(
      age ~ "Age (years)",
      gender ~ "Gender",
      bmi ~ "Body Mass Index",
      vaccine ~ "Vaccination Status",
      SBP ~ "Systolic Blood Pressure",
      LDL ~ "LDL Cholesterol"
    )
  ) %>%
  add_p() %>% 
  modify_header(label = "**Variable**") %>%
  bold_labels()
Variable not_severe

2 Wilcoxon rank sum test; Pearson’s Chi-squared test 
# Combined Exploratory Figure
featurePlot(x = my_dat[, c("age", "bmi", "SBP", "LDL", "depression")], 
            y = my_dat$severity, 
            plot = "box", 
            scales = list(x = list(relation = "free"), y = list(relation = "free")),
            main = "Distribution of Clinical Factors by Severity")

Training Simpler Models

Logistic Regression

# CV Control
ctrl <- trainControl(method = "cv", number = 10, 
                     summaryFunction = twoClassSummary, 
                     classProbs = TRUE)

# Baseline: Simple Logistic Regression (GLM) 
set.seed(3015)
model.glm <- train(severity ~ ., data = training_data,
                   method = "glm",
                   metric = "ROC",
                   trControl = ctrl)

summary(model.glm$finalModel)
## 
## Call:
## NULL
## 
## Coefficients:
##                     Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)       -57.962492  31.520601  -1.839   0.0659 .  
## age                 0.113228   0.026571   4.261 2.03e-05 ***
## genderMale         -0.269848   0.205795  -1.311   0.1898    
## raceAsian          -0.249548   0.442122  -0.564   0.5725    
## raceBlack          -0.211653   0.253922  -0.834   0.4045    
## raceHispanic        0.786331   0.353445   2.225   0.0261 *  
## smokingFormer      -0.102801   0.237211  -0.433   0.6647    
## smokingCurrent      0.162092   0.350890   0.462   0.6441    
## height              0.190594   0.183937   1.036   0.3001    
## weight             -0.178100   0.193855  -0.919   0.3582    
## bmi                 0.730206   0.552980   1.320   0.1867    
## diabetesYes         0.178137   0.285286   0.624   0.5324    
## hypertensionYes    -0.124906   0.342276  -0.365   0.7152    
## SBP                 0.112500   0.023551   4.777 1.78e-06 ***
## LDL                -0.008110   0.005335  -1.520   0.1284    
## vaccineVaccinated  -3.430038   0.246406 -13.920  < 2e-16 ***
## depression         -0.004871   0.049416  -0.099   0.9215    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## (Dispersion parameter for binomial family taken to be 1)
## 
##     Null deviance: 1010.39  on 799  degrees of freedom
## Residual deviance:  614.61  on 783  degrees of freedom
## AIC: 648.61
## 
## Number of Fisher Scoring iterations: 5

Penalized Logistic Regression (Elastic Net)

glmnGrid <- expand.grid(.alpha = seq(0, 1, length = 21),
                        .lambda = exp(seq(-8, -1, length = 50)))
set.seed(3015)
model.enet <- train(severity ~ ., data = training_data,
                    method = "glmnet",
                    tuneGrid = glmnGrid,
                    metric = "ROC",
                    trControl = ctrl)

model.enet$bestTune
##     alpha     lambda
## 582  0.55 0.02811566
myCol = rainbow(25)
myPar = list(superpose.symbol = list(col = myCol),
             superpose.line = list(col = myCol))

plot(model.enet , par.settings = myPar, xTrans = log)

Multivariate Adaptive Regression Splines (MARS)

set.seed(3015)
model.mars <- train(severity ~ ., 
                    data = training_data,
                    method = "earth", 
                    tuneGrid = expand.grid(degree = 1:4, nprune = 2:20),
                    metric = "ROC",
                    trControl = ctrl)

Generalized Additive Model (GAM)

set.seed(3015)
model.gam <- train(severity ~ .,
                   data = training_data,
                   method = "gam", 
                   metric = "ROC", 
                   trControl = ctrl)
## Loading required package: mgcv
## Loading required package: nlme
## 
## Attaching package: 'nlme'
## The following object is masked from 'package:dplyr':
## 
##     collapse
## This is mgcv 1.9-3. For overview type 'help("mgcv-package")'.
## 
## Attaching package: 'mgcv'
## The following objects are masked from 'package:gam':
## 
##     gam, gam.control, gam.fit, s
model.gam$bestTune
##   select method
## 2   TRUE GCV.Cp
model.gam$finalModel
## 
## Family: binomial 
## Link function: logit 
## 
## Formula:
## .outcome ~ genderMale + raceAsian + raceBlack + raceHispanic + 
##     smokingFormer + smokingCurrent + diabetesYes + hypertensionYes + 
##     vaccineVaccinated + s(depression) + s(age) + s(SBP) + s(LDL) + 
##     s(bmi) + s(height) + s(weight)
## 
## Estimated degrees of freedom:
## 0.0001 3.2826 1.9058 0.5612 1.7815 0.0001 0.3668 
##  total = 17.9 
## 
## UBRE score: -0.1987336
plot(model.gam$finalModel, se = TRUE)

summary(model.gam$finalModel)
## 
## Family: binomial 
## Link function: logit 
## 
## Formula:
## .outcome ~ genderMale + raceAsian + raceBlack + raceHispanic + 
##     smokingFormer + smokingCurrent + diabetesYes + hypertensionYes + 
##     vaccineVaccinated + s(depression) + s(age) + s(SBP) + s(LDL) + 
##     s(bmi) + s(height) + s(weight)
## 
## Parametric coefficients:
##                   Estimate Std. Error z value Pr(>|z|)    
## (Intercept)        0.98829    0.26816   3.685 0.000228 ***
## genderMale        -0.25629    0.20522  -1.249 0.211715    
## raceAsian         -0.24618    0.43933  -0.560 0.575244    
## raceBlack         -0.20837    0.25483  -0.818 0.413529    
## raceHispanic       0.80011    0.35232   2.271 0.023147 *  
## smokingFormer     -0.07053    0.23684  -0.298 0.765854    
## smokingCurrent     0.14494    0.35305   0.411 0.681405    
## diabetesYes        0.19005    0.28469   0.668 0.504400    
## hypertensionYes   -0.18716    0.36637  -0.511 0.609448    
## vaccineVaccinated -3.39890    0.24104 -14.101  < 2e-16 ***
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## Approximate significance of smooth terms:
##                     edf Ref.df Chi.sq  p-value    
## s(depression) 6.355e-05      9  0.000    0.946    
## s(age)        3.283e+00      9 21.073 3.82e-05 ***
## s(SBP)        1.906e+00      9 23.144 1.98e-06 ***
## s(LDL)        5.612e-01      9  1.185    0.145    
## s(bmi)        1.781e+00      9 18.000 1.73e-06 ***
## s(height)     1.032e-04      9  0.000    0.599    
## s(weight)     3.668e-01      9  0.780    0.127    
## ---
## Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
## 
## R-sq.(adj) =  0.459   Deviance explained = 40.1%
## UBRE = -0.19873  Scale est. = 1         n = 800
par(mfrow = c(2, 2)) 


plot(model.gam$finalModel, 
     shade = TRUE, 
     seWithMean = TRUE, 
     main = "Clinical Nonlinear Trends (2021-2023)", 
     col = "blue", 
     pch = 16)

par(mfrow = c(1, 1))

Classification Tree

grid.tree <- expand.grid(cp = seq(0.0001, 0.05, length = 50))

set.seed(3015) # Using your consistent project seed
model.tree <- train(severity ~ ., 
                   data = training_data, 
                   method = "rpart", 
                   metric = "ROC", 
                   tuneGrid = grid.tree, 
                   trControl = ctrl)

model.tree
## CART 
## 
## 800 samples
##  13 predictor
##   2 classes: 'not_severe', 'severe' 
## 
## No pre-processing
## Resampling: Cross-Validated (10 fold) 
## Summary of sample sizes: 720, 719, 721, 720, 720, 720, ...

## ROC was used to select the optimal model using the largest value.
## The final value used for the model was cp = 0.002136735.
plot(model.tree)

rpart.plot(model.tree$finalModel, type = 4, extra = 101, under = TRUE, cex = 0.8,
           main = "Decision Tree for COVID-19 Severity")

Training Advanced models

# Boosting (GBM) 
gbmA.grid <- expand.grid(n.trees = c(100, 500, 1000, 2000, 5000), 
                        interaction.depth = 1:5, 
                        shrinkage = c(0.001, 0.003, 0.005),
                        n.minobsinnode = 10) 
set.seed(3015)
model.gbm <- train(severity ~ ., 
                   data = training_data, 
                   method = "gbm",
                   distribution = "adaboost",
                   tuneGrid = gbmA.grid,
                   metric = "ROC",
                   trControl = ctrl, 
                   verbose = FALSE) 

ggplot(model.gbm, highlight = TRUE)

# SVM with Radial Kernel 
svmr.grid <- expand.grid(C = exp(seq(1, 7, len = 20)), 
                         sigma = exp(seq(-10, -2, len = 10))) 
set.seed(3015)
model.svm <- train(severity ~ ., 
                    data = training_data, 
                    method = "svmRadialSigma",
                    tuneGrid = svmr.grid, 
                    metric = "ROC", 
                    trControl = ctrl) 
myCol <- rainbow(25)
myPar <- list(superpose.symbol = list(col = myCol),
superpose.line = list(col = myCol))
plot(model.svm, highlight = TRUE, par.settings = myPar)

Tuning visual for all models with grids

# Tuning plots 
# Elastic Net tuning plot 
p_enet <- plot(model.enet, par.settings = myPar, xTrans = log, main = "Enet Tuning")

# MARS tuning plot 
p_mars <- ggplot(model.mars) + ggtitle("MARS Tuning")

# Classification Tree
p_tree <- ggplot(model.tree, highlight = TRUE) + ggtitle("Decision Tree Tuning")

# Boosting tuning plot 
p_gbm <- ggplot(model.gbm, highlight = TRUE) + ggtitle("Boosting Tuning")

# SVM tuning plot 
p_svm <- plot(model.svm, highlight = TRUE, par.settings = myPar, main = "SVM Tuning")

# Grid
grid.arrange(p_enet, p_mars, p_tree, p_gbm, p_svm, 
             nrow = 3, ncol = 2,
             top = "Figure: Model Selection and Hyperparameter Tuning")

# Variable Importance Plot for the Advanced Models
vip(model.gbm, method = "model") + ggtitle("Key Factors for COVID-19 Severity (Boosting)")

# Partial Dependence Plots for most important continuous variables
# p1 - Age
p1 <- pdp::partial(model.gbm, pred.var = "age", grid.resolution = 100, prob = TRUE) %>%
  autoplot(rug = TRUE, train = training_data) + 
  ggtitle("Influence of Age") +
  ylab("Prob(Severe)")

# p2 -  Systolic Blood Pressure (SBP)
p2 <- pdp::partial(model.gbm, pred.var = "SBP", grid.resolution = 100, prob = TRUE) %>%
  autoplot(rug = TRUE, train = training_data) + 
  ggtitle("Influence of SBP") +
  ylab("Prob(Severe)")

# p3 - Body Mass Index (BMI)
p3 <- pdp::partial(model.gbm, pred.var = "bmi", grid.resolution = 100, prob = TRUE) %>%
  autoplot(rug = TRUE, train = training_data) + 
  ggtitle("Influence of BMI") +
  ylab("Prob(Severe)")

# p4 - Vaccination status (binary)
p_vac <- pdp::partial(model.gbm, 
                      pred.var = "vaccine", 
                      which.class = 2, # Tells R to plot the 'severe' class
                      prob = TRUE) %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = vaccine, y = yhat, fill = vaccine)) +
  geom_col(width = 0.5) +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Marginal Effect of Vaccination on COVID-19 Severity",
       y = "Predicted Probability of Severe Infection",
       x = "Vaccination Status") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c("Unvaccinated" = "firebrick", "Vaccinated" = "forestgreen"))

# Combining
grid.arrange(p1, p2, p3, p_vac, nrow = 1)

Internal Model Comparison

# Compare all trained models 
resamp <- resamples(list(
  GLM = model.glm, 
  ENET = model.enet, 
  MARS = model.mars, 
  TREE = model.tree,
  GAM = model.gam, 
  Boosting = model.gbm, 
  SVM = model.svm
)) 

# Summarize the performance metrics (ROC, Sens, Spec) across resamples
summary(resamp) 
## 
## Call:
## summary.resamples(object = resamp)
## 
## Models: GLM, ENET, MARS, TREE, GAM, Boosting, SVM 
## Number of resamples: 10 
## 
## ROC 
##               Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## GLM      0.7997257 0.8365183 0.8810541 0.8754061 0.9147080 0.9423077    0
## ENET     0.7969822 0.8356146 0.8860399 0.8802398 0.9319801 0.9344729    0
## MARS     0.7911523 0.8370391 0.8865741 0.8779474 0.9197828 0.9444444    0
## TREE     0.7945816 0.8096661 0.8217593 0.8414972 0.8749110 0.9326923    0
## GAM      0.7956104 0.8246318 0.8675214 0.8679782 0.9209402 0.9309117    0
## Boosting 0.8034979 0.8407599 0.8851496 0.8793338 0.9194266 0.9351852    0
## SVM      0.7990398 0.8347545 0.8782051 0.8752502 0.9216524 0.9351852    0
## 
## Sens 
##               Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## GLM      0.8301887 0.8703704 0.8796296 0.8848707 0.9027778 0.9259259    0
## ENET     0.8301887 0.8750000 0.8981481 0.8941300 0.9074074 0.9444444    0
## MARS     0.7924528 0.8750000 0.8888889 0.8829490 0.9074074 0.9259259    0
## TREE     0.7924528 0.8564815 0.8888889 0.8755416 0.9074074 0.9074074    0
## GAM      0.8113208 0.8703704 0.8888889 0.8755765 0.8888889 0.9074074    0
## Boosting 0.7547170 0.8564815 0.8796296 0.8791754 0.9074074 0.9629630    0
## SVM      0.6226415 0.7824074 0.8148148 0.7974493 0.8425926 0.8703704    0
## 
## Spec 
##               Min.   1st Qu.    Median      Mean   3rd Qu.      Max. NA's
## GLM      0.5925926 0.7019231 0.7307692 0.7169516 0.7596154 0.8076923    0
## ENET     0.5000000 0.6923077 0.7307692 0.7092593 0.7692308 0.8461538    0
## MARS     0.5000000 0.6730769 0.7500000 0.7205128 0.7980769 0.8461538    0
## TREE     0.5769231 0.6153846 0.6538462 0.6707977 0.7211538 0.8461538    0
## GAM      0.5000000 0.6634615 0.7115385 0.7054131 0.7692308 0.8461538    0
## Boosting 0.5555556 0.6153846 0.7115385 0.6901709 0.7692308 0.8076923    0
## SVM      0.6296296 0.7692308 0.8076923 0.7898860 0.8076923 0.9230769    0
# Visualize the ROC performance comparison
bwplot(resamp, metric = "ROC")

Model Evaluation of top performers on Test Set

# Final validation on the Test Set for ALL models
probs_glm   <- predict(model.glm,   newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_enet  <- predict(model.enet,  newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_mars  <- predict(model.mars,  newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_tree  <- predict(model.tree,  newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_gam   <- predict(model.gam,   newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_gbm   <- predict(model.gbm,   newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]
probs_svm   <- predict(model.svm,   newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]

roc_glm_final   <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_glm)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_enet_final  <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_enet)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_mars_final  <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_mars)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_tree_final  <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_tree)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_gam_final   <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_gam)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_gbm_final   <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_gbm)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
roc_svm_final   <- roc(testing_data$severity, probs_svm)
## Setting levels: control = not_severe, case = severe
## Setting direction: controls < cases
# Comparison Plot
roc_list_final <- list(
  GLM = roc_glm_final, 
  ENET = roc_enet_final, 
  MARS = roc_mars_final,
  Tree = roc_tree_final,
  GAM = roc_gam_final, 
  Boosting = roc_gbm_final, 
  SVM = roc_svm_final
)

p_final_roc <- ggroc(roc_list_final, legacy.axes = TRUE) +
  geom_abline(intercept = 0, slope = 1, color = "grey", linetype = "dashed") +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(title = "Final Test Set Performance Comparison (All Models)",
       x = "1 - Specificity (False Positive Rate)",
       y = "Sensitivity (True Positive Rate)") +
  scale_color_discrete(name = "Model")


p_final_roc

# Final AUC values
get_roc_stats <- function(roc_obj) {
  stats <- coords(roc_obj, "best", ret = c("sensitivity", "specificity"), transpose = FALSE)
  return(stats[1, ]) 
}

auc_table_detailed <- data.frame(
  Model = c("Baseline GLM", "Elastic Net", "MARS", "Decision Tree", "GAM", "Boosting", "SVM"),
  Test_AUC = c(as.numeric(auc(roc_glm_final)), 
               as.numeric(auc(roc_enet_final)), 
               as.numeric(auc(roc_mars_final)), 
               as.numeric(auc(roc_tree_final)),
               as.numeric(auc(roc_gam_final)), 
               as.numeric(auc(roc_gbm_final)), 
               as.numeric(auc(roc_svm_final)))
)

roc_list <- list(roc_glm_final, roc_enet_final, roc_mars_final, 
                 roc_tree_final, roc_gam_final, roc_gbm_final, roc_svm_final)

metrics_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(roc_list, get_roc_stats))
auc_table_detailed <- cbind(auc_table_detailed, metrics_df)

auc_table_detailed %>%
  arrange(desc(Test_AUC)) %>%
  kable(caption = "Table 3: Final Model Performance on Unseen Test Set (n=200)",
        col.names = c("Model", "Test AUC", "Sensitivity", "Specificity"),
        digits = 4, 
        booktabs = TRUE,
        align = "lccc") %>%
  kable_styling(bootstrap_options = c("striped", "hover"), full_width = F) %>%
  row_spec(1, bold = TRUE, background = "#DFF0D8")
Table 3: Final Model Performance on Unseen Test Set (n=200)
Model Test AUC Sensitivity Specificity
MARS 0.9218 0.8710 0.8551
GAM 0.9188 0.8871 0.8261
SVM 0.9173 0.8548 0.8261
Elastic Net 0.9171 0.8710 0.8261
Boosting 0.9136 0.8226 0.8913
Baseline GLM 0.9127 0.8387 0.8696
Decision Tree 0.8790 0.7258 0.9058 
  auc_table_detailed
##           Model  Test_AUC sensitivity specificity
## 1  Baseline GLM 0.9126928   0.8387097   0.8695652
## 2   Elastic Net 0.9171342   0.8709677   0.8260870
## 3          MARS 0.9218093   0.8709677   0.8550725
## 4 Decision Tree 0.8790323   0.7258065   0.9057971
## 5           GAM 0.9187705   0.8870968   0.8260870
## 6      Boosting 0.9135694   0.8225806   0.8913043
## 7           SVM 0.9172511   0.8548387   0.8260870

Predictive Risk Score

# Generate the risk scores (probabilities) using the best-performing model (MARS)
test_risk_scores <- predict(model.mars, newdata = testing_data, type = "prob")[, "severe"]

risk_score_report <- testing_data %>%
  mutate(Risk_Score = round(test_risk_scores, 4)) %>%
  select(age, gender, vaccine, bmi, Risk_Score)

# Display the first 10 participants to show example of the individual risk quantification
head(risk_score_report, 10)
##    age gender      vaccine  bmi Risk_Score
## 1   63   Male Unvaccinated 27.8     0.8175
## 2   60   Male   Vaccinated 23.6     0.0116
## 3   64 Female Unvaccinated 27.9     0.7512
## 4   60 Female   Vaccinated 29.7     0.1743
## 5   57 Female Unvaccinated 22.6     0.2716
## 6   61 Female   Vaccinated 26.9     0.0684
## 7   69   Male Unvaccinated 26.4     0.7808
## 8   56   Male Unvaccinated 27.1     0.4721
## 9   56   Male   Vaccinated 25.1     0.0176
## 10  55   Male Unvaccinated 30.3     0.7204
# Creating a table to show the predictions by age groupings and vaccination status
risk_summary_data <- risk_score_report %>%
  mutate(
    age_group = cut(age, breaks = c(48, 65, 100), 
                    labels = c( "Middle-Aged (48-65)", "Senior (65+)")),
    Risk_Percent = Risk_Score * 100 # Convert to percentage for readability
  )

#  tbl_summary
risk_summary_table <- risk_summary_data %>%
  select(age_group, vaccine, gender, Risk_Percent) %>%
  tbl_summary(
    by = vaccine,
    include = c(age_group, gender, Risk_Percent),
    statistic = list(Risk_Percent ~ "{mean}% ({min}%, {max}%)"),
    label = list(
      age_group ~ "Age Category",
      gender ~ "Gender",
      Risk_Percent ~ "Predicted Risk of Severity (Mean, Range)"
    )
  ) %>%
  modify_header(label = "**Patient Characteristics**") %>%
  modify_spanning_header(all_stat_cols() ~ "**Predicted Risk by Vaccination Status**") %>%
  bold_labels()


risk_summary_table
